name: Gmailtrigger Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Gmailtrigger Workflow. This workflow integrates 8 different services: stickyNote, wait, gmailTrigger, gmailTool, agent. It contains 14 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.notes: This gmailTool node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Gmail labelling agent
nodes.notes:
Objective:
Automatically categorize incoming emails based on existing Gmail labels or create a new label if none match.

Tools:
- Get message
- Read all labels
- Create label
- Assign label to message

Instructions:

Label Matching:

Analyze the email's subject, sender, recipient, keywords, and content.
Compare with existing Gmail labels to find the most relevant match.
Label Assignment:

Assign the email to the most appropriate existing label.`
Remove the inbox label if the email is of less importance (like ads, promotions, aka "Reclame"), keep normal and important emails in the inbox.
If no suitable label exists, create a new label based on the existing labels. Try reusing existing labels as much as possible. Always create a label as a sublabel, if no label applies, if the main label already exists, create the new label under the existing label, if no main label exists, create the label AI and create the new label under this label.
Label Creation:

Ensure new labels align with the structure of existing ones, including capitalization, delimiters, and prefixes.
Examples:

If the email subject is "Project Alpha Update," assign to [Project Alpha] if it exists.
For "New Vendor Inquiry," create "Vendor Inquiry" if no relevant label exists.
Outcome:
Emails are consistently categorized under the appropriate or newly created labels, maintaining Gmail's organizational structure.

parameters.text:

{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Gmail Trigger".
Open the "OpenAI Chat Model1" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Gmail - read labels" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Gmail - get message" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Gmail - add label to message" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Gmail - create label" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Gmail labelling agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Window Buffer Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Wait" node (n8n-nodes-base.wait) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
